div1.entryFree:
Hood, vb. 1) to dress in a cowl: Meas. V, 358.
2) to cover so as to bar sight; originally a term of falconry, as the hawk was hooded till let fly at the game: h. mine eyes thus with my hat, Merch. II, 2, 202. 'tis a --ed valour, and when it appears, it will bate, H5 III, 7, 121. h. my unmanned blood, Rom. III, 2, 14.
